claim_boundary: TICKET120 proves an elementary universal pair-saving identity and refutes a weak-contract fixed-fraction lemma. The scale rows are finite diagnostics. It does not prove a Vaughan arithmetic angle gap, eventual endpoint closure, or the Twin Prime Conjecture.

exact_identity.combined: singleton low-pair budget - paired low-pair budget = scalar saving + centered saving >= 0
exact_identity.scope: universal for two scalar means and two vectors in a real or complex Hilbert space

abstract_countermodels.purpose: refutes every universal fixed positive pair-saving fraction under only scalar triangle and positive-semidefinite Gram hypotheses
shows that the same weak contract permits complete cancellation and therefore does not determine an arithmetic saving rate

discarded_candidate.name: UniversalFixedFractionLowDivisorPairSavingUnderGramContract
discarded_candidate.claim: there exists eta>0 such that paired_budget <= (1-eta) singleton_budget under only the triangle and positive-semidefinite Gram contracts
discarded_candidate.reason: the witness has positive-semidefinite rank-one Gram matrix and paired/singleton ratio exactly one

retained_theorem.name: VaughanLowDivisorDenominatorSummedAngleGap
retained_theorem.quantifiers: there exist eta>0 and X0 such that every X>=X0 satisfies an arithmetic denominator-summed mean-sign and centered-angle gap for the actual first canonical Vaughan pair
retained_theorem.required_input: signed Mobius/divisor convolution, denominator phases, and scale-uniform coefficient estimates not implied by Gram positivity
retained_theorem.counterexample_route: construct an unbounded Vaughan-realizable sequence whose low-pair paired/singleton ratio approaches one or whose paired-to-known contribution defeats every fixed full-budget margin

twin_low_divisor_pair_savings_audit.proof_boundary: TICKET120 proves an elementary universal pair-saving identity and refutes a weak-contract fixed-fraction lemma. The scale rows are finite diagnostics. It does not prove a Vaughan arithmetic angle gap, eventual endpoint closure, or the Twin Prime Conjecture.

attempts.proof_or_counterexample_mode: prove the exact weak identity, then falsify unjustified uniform strengthening
attempts.attempt: Decompose the first canonical pair saving into scalar sign and centered Hilbert-angle terms at every denominator, then test whether Gram positivity alone forces a fixed fractional saving.
attempts.obstruction: The exact aligned rank-one countermodel has zero pair saving, so any positive uniform saving must use arithmetic Vaughan structure absent from the weak Gram contract.
attempts.next_experiment: Expand the first factor-four pair into signed Mobius/divisor sums and seek a denominator-summed arithmetic angle gap, while using the aligned model as the mandatory falsification oracle.
attempts.claim_boundary: No Twin Prime proof and no certified last-twin counterexample.
